demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The pain hit me out of nowhere and feels like it's boring straight through to my spine. I can't keep a sip of water down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly about four hours ago and is really severe, and I keep vomiting so I can't drink anything to settle it.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, about four hours ago, out of nowhere
symptom_narrative.duration: around four hours and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: severe pain high up in the middle of my belly that bores straight through to my back
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain, constant, I can't get comfortable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: high in the middle of my tummy, under the ribs, going right through to my spine
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not coming in waves
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: it eases a little if I sit up and lean forward
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying flat, and trying to eat or drink
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: awful nausea and I keep vomiting; I can't keep even a sip of water down; I feel sweaty and shaky
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed curled up; I can't stand up straight or do anything

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I've been told I have gallstones
past_medical_history.how_managed: no operation for them, just known about

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: I don't really drink; I haven't had much alcohol
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse is here with me and can help, but has no medical training

objective_data_offered.value: curled up / sitting leaning forward because it eases the pain a little
looks pale, sweaty and distressed, holding the upper belly
repeated vomiting, cannot keep even a sip of water down; the vomit looks like stomach contents, no blood seen
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: spouse observation, not a clinician assessment
